name: Project — LinkedIn Build Log Series (OpenClaw journey)
summary: A series of LinkedIn posts documenting the OpenClaw setup and all shipped projects (wins, bugs, fixes, learnings).
architecture: Research best practices for LinkedIn writing, define a repeatable post template/skill, then draft and publish a multi-post narrative with links and screenshots.
nextSteps: Collect timeline + key milestones across projects
Research LinkedIn post best practices (hook, formatting, cadence)
Draft 6–10 posts with a posting plan/order
Package a reusable skill/template for future LinkedIn updates

name: Camera Relay + Last Hour Recorder
summary: Proxy the Android IP Webcam stream through the Mac so it stays reachable over Tailscale and keep a rolling 1-hour MP4 buffer.
architecture: Node/Express relay (8090) proxies IP Webcam endpoints. FFmpeg daemon records 60 x 1-minute clips into ~/Videos/ipwebcam-last-hour and server.js exposes /recordings metadata and raw files.
nextSteps: Add HLS playlist so phone can scrub the last hour
Turn recorder into LaunchAgent with restart logic
Optionally add systemd-style health endpoint

name: Stock Trading Dashboard
summary: Real-time market simulator + paper-trading bot with guardrails. UI has a persistent top status banner that explains why the bot is blocked/paused (market/news errors, stale data wall, daily loss wall) and shows connection failures.
architecture: Express server serves React-ish UI + API endpoints. Background loop fetches prices (Yahoo/stooq), logs metrics, and scaffolds a bot with configurable guards.
nextSteps: Replace remaining UI alerts with banner messages (single consistent error surface)
Add a compact debug line to the banner (bot ON/OFF, daily PnL %, total PnL %, data source, staleness)
Verify on mobile Safari/Chrome with cache-busting (?v=...) and bot toggle flows

name: Project HQ Dashboard
summary: Single pane for every active/planned project with links, description, and shortcut to ping this Telegram bot.
architecture: Static JSON drives a vanilla JS SPA; each card opens details and includes Telegram share button pre-filling the project name.
nextSteps: Add ability to tag projects by priority/owner
Include heartbeat of external services (relay status, dashboard uptime)
Extend Telegram action to log state or start timers

name: Spider-Groove Flight
summary: Neon Flappy-Bird tribute where Spider-Man swings through pylons; responsive on mobile and deployed via GitHub Pages.
architecture: Canvas + JS loop handles gravity, jumping, and obstacle spawning. Body/touch listeners limited to the canvas so scrolling stays intact and the physics stay smooth.
nextSteps: Add new obstacle patterns
Refine neon trail FX
Hit analytics/logging for high scores

name: Rainbow Hop
summary: Kid-friendly vertical jumper with Hebrew guides, sparkly platforms, and celebratory overlays.
architecture: Single-page JS game with jump physics, overlay modals for game over/high scores, and touch/mouse handlers scoped to the canvas so scroll keeps working.
nextSteps: Add power-ups (double jump)
Surface Hebrew copy variations
Track best runs via localStorage

name: Project 1 — Kids’ English Coach
summary: Hebrew-first web app for a 7yo: English word + audio + 4 Hebrew options (no nikud) + per-user progress. Live on Vercel (auth working).
architecture: Next.js App Router + Tailwind UI. Auth + Postgres via Supabase (profiles + attempts tables, RLS) with proper email redirect callback route (/auth/callback) on the deployed domain. Deployed on Vercel. Deck-based quiz + admin create-user route shipped; UI is pastel & kid-friendly.
nextSteps: Run Supabase migration: add attempts.app_id (see repo supabase-migrations) so multiple apps can share auth
Promote Sheffer to admin in profiles.role (DB-backed admin, remove email shortcut later)
Harden admin: restrict /api/admin/create-user to admin role
Add visual deck cards (instead of dropdown) + small animations
Expand Level 2 content (verbs/adjectives)

name: Project 3 — Spoken Adventure (English Acquisition 5–6)
summary: Scene-based Spoken Adventure live: world map (4 worlds), Pirate Bay interactive hotspots, local progression, reusable scene components, animations, and autoplay ambient audio.
architecture: Next.js app-router + Tailwind. Scene engine keeps SEE/HEAR/DO/SAY flow while hiding stepper UI. LocalStorage progression unlocks worlds. Pirate Bay includes hotspot interactions (find key, chest tap/drag open, ordered follow path), placeholder SVG character, and reusable scene frame/animation utilities.
nextSteps: Build the remaining three worlds (Dino Jungle, Space Lab, Magic Castle) with scene-specific interactions
Add persistence sync to Supabase with app-scoped profile progress
Add optional mic-based SAY scoring and kid-friendly feedback effects

name: Project 2 — AI Tech Lead Digest
summary: Twice-weekly AI newsletter (Tue+Fri 09:30 Asia/Jerusalem) + keyword-based breaking alerts. Telegram headlines link to an HTML issue page with curated summaries and source links.
architecture: Local generator builds static HTML issues + archive index (GitHub Pages). Scheduled via macOS LaunchAgents (zero-token idle). Breaking alerts poll RSS hourly with keyword filters and send to Telegram.
nextSteps: Tune RSS source list (add/remove feeds, reduce noise) and add categories (Models/Agents/Tooling)
Improve HTML issue design: story cards with TL;DR + Why-it-matters + Action (TRY/TRACK/IGNORE)
Update Telegram message to list top headlines (each points to the issue page, plus original links inside)

name: PanelView Survey Autopilot
summary: Zero-token automation: watches for new PanelView survey invite emails, notifies on Telegram with the link, then we can open/fill the survey via Chrome Relay (neutral defaults) with human review before final submit.
architecture: macOS LaunchAgent polls Gmail via local gws OAuth; dedupes by messageId; sends Telegram notification via openclaw CLI; optional Chrome Relay flow to open and fill surveys.
nextSteps: When a new invite arrives, run the Chrome Relay flow to fill it (neutral answers by default), then ask for final submit confirmation
Improve link picking heuristics if PanelView email templates change
(Optional) Add a manual 'run now' button/command to force-check and notify

name: Shopping List (Hebrew) — Telegram + Shopping Mode
summary: Hebrew shopping list via dedicated Telegram group + shopping-mode web app. Core flows work (add/list/start shopping, in-cart toggle, finish trip), UI significantly upgraded, and persistence moved to dedicated Supabase project. Remaining issue: intermittent invalid_session from mixed token sources; plan is dedicated Telegram bot tomorrow.
architecture: Vercel API + Vercel Web (RTL SPA). Persistence on dedicated Supabase project upfbcgsaekrebxmtkpsq. Telegram group ingestion via OpenClaw group allowlist/commands today; migration to dedicated Telegram bot + direct webhook planned for stability.
nextSteps: Create dedicated Telegram bot for shopping app and connect direct webhook to API (remove mixed token paths).
Finalize token source to always use API /api/shopping/start and prevent invalid_session links.
Implement image-to-item OCR flow with approve/edit/cancel in Telegram.
Add lightweight sync indicator ("סונכרן עכשיו") and optional optimistic UI for cart toggles.
Optional: migrate web to Next.js for stronger cache/version control.

done: Dedicated Supabase project created and connected: upfbcgsaekrebxmtkpsq
API persistence switched from local SQLite to Supabase-backed logic
Full reset endpoint added for clean starts during development
Added category collapse, hide-in-cart mode, search, sticky summary bar
Added live sync polling (3s) and no-store cache-busting on GET requests
latestUpdate.currentIssue: Some links still resolve to invalid_session when token comes from a stale/mixed path; fresh API-generated verified links work.
latestUpdate.plan: Tomorrow: dedicate separate Telegram bot + direct webhook for this app.

name: WCA DB Query
summary: Build an LLM/RAG-powered interface over the World Cube Association results database so the team can ask natural questions and return structured answers.
architecture: Frontend (React/Next) + RAG agent + backend query service. Source data from https://www.worldcubeassociation.org/export/results and optionally cache locally in Supabase or SQLite.
nextSteps: Catalog relevant tables (competitions, records, competitors).
Prototype RAG agent that translates natural language into SQL queries against the WCA data.
Design UI with data table + prompts + update workflow.
Plan low-cost token strategy (local embeddings + LLM only for query generation).
